stories.title: Chick-fil-A security incident may have exposed some customer account data
stories.summary: Chick-fil-A has notified potentially affected customers after a security incident may have exposed personal information linked to a limited number of customer loyalty accounts, including names, email addresses, and last four digits of credit or debit card numbers. The incident occurred between July 17 and 19, 2026, when unauthorized parties used account credentials to launch an attack on the company's website and mobile apps. Chick-fil-A has taken steps to secure affected accounts and is communicating directly with impacted customers. The company is committed to maintaining customers' trust.

stories.title: Tesla Autopilot and FSD crashes hit record 207 in a single month
stories.summary: Tesla reported 207 crashes involving its Autopilot and Full Self-Driving systems in May 2026, the highest single-month total on record. This surpasses the 157 crashes recorded in all of 2021. The crashes meet a severity threshold, including airbag deployments, injuries, or fatalities. The company now accounts for 3,763 unique driver-assist crashes, nearly 85 percent of the industry's total.

stories.title: Global Meta outage disrupts Facebook and Instagram services worldwide
stories.summary: Thousands of Facebook and Instagram users worldwide experienced service outages on Sunday due to a Meta platform disruption, with users reporting login failures, inaccessible accounts, and difficulties loading content. The cause of the outage remains unclear. Approximately 1 in 5 users were affected, with some users unable to access new content, including Reels and posts. Meta is working to resolve the issue.

stories.title: Hello Sunshine’s Head of TV and Film on 10 Years of Reese Witherspoon’s Production Company, ‘Big Little Lies’ Season 3, Apple’s ‘Lucky’ and More ‘Elle’
stories.summary: Reese Witherspoon's production company, Hello Sunshine, is releasing two high-profile shows this summer. A prequel series "Elle" based on the movie "Legally Blonde" debuts on Amazon's Prime Video in July, followed by the action miniseries "Lucky" starring Anya Taylor-Joy on Apple TV. Both shows are produced by Hello Sunshine, marking a significant milestone for the company. The releases come as Hello Sunshine celebrates 10 years of producing content.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The Reds are reportedly beating Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Barcola's potential move to Liverpool could be finalized soon. Meanwhile, talks are ongoing for Bruno Fernandes to commit his future to Manchester United.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US has signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the country to enrich uranium on its soil for the first time. The deal, worth billions of dollars, will be implemented at a US-built enrichment facility, advancing US and regional security. The agreement upholds high standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation, with US companies involved in the project. The deal is expected to strengthen the US competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

stories.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Screenplay Is Now Available to Buy Online
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan's screenplay for "The Odyssey" is now available for purchase online, priced at $13.95 for a 240-page paperback. The official release date is August 4. The film, starring Matt Damon, has collected $124.5 million domestically and $264.1 million worldwide. The screenplay offers readers a closer look at Nolan's adaptation of Homer's "The Odyssey" for the screen.

stories.title: Louvre Gallery Reopens After Brazen Jewel Heist
stories.summary: The Louvre Museum's Apollo Gallery reopened on Wednesday after a nine-month closure following a brazen jewel heist in October, in which thieves stole eight precious crown jewels worth over $100 million. The gallery, known for its extensive collection, felt strangely empty without the centerpiece display cases. Visitors were happy to return, but expressed disappointment at the absence of the stolen items. The museum's security measures are now under scrutiny.

stories.title: Darren Aronofsky’s AI Studio Primordial Soup Raising $15 Million
stories.summary: Darren Aronofsky's AI studio Primordial Soup is seeking to raise $15 million in cash through a Securities and Exchange Commission filing. The studio, launched a year ago, has already sold over $11 million in equity this month. As part of its expansion, Primordial Soup is hiring for key positions, including a "Head of Studio" and "Generative Artists." The studio's AI-focused projects are being produced in partnership with Google's DeepMind unit.

stories.title: Fire kills 10 members of same family in Peru, police say
stories.summary: A devastating fire in Lima, Peru, has claimed the lives of 10 members of the same family, including five children, and two others were injured. The blaze occurred at a home in the La Victoria district early Wednesday, with 17 family members present at the time. Five children and four adults were killed, with seven family members managing to escape. Police are investigating reports that the fire was started by arsonists.

stories.title: Miami mistakenly post James 'introductory' video
stories.summary: Miami Heat mistakenly posted a link to LeBron James' introductory press conference on YouTube, scheduled for July 27, before deleting it. The error occurred as staff prepared for the possibility of James joining the team. LeBron James, a 41-year-old 22-time All-Star, is considering where to play next season after leaving the Los Angeles Lakers in June. His potential return to the Heat would form a formidable starting five alongside Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo.

stories.title: Captain Salt leads Fire to victory against Brave
stories.summary: Welsh Fire captain Phil Salt led his team to a six-wicket victory over Southern Brave in the Hundred men's competition, scoring an unbeaten 47 off 37 balls. The win came at the expense of Brave's 135-7 total, with Salt's 47 helping Fire reach 138-4. This marks Fire's first win of the 2026 Hundred season. Fire will look to build on this momentum as they continue their campaign.

stories.title: Why Scottie Scheffler believes Jordan Spieth withdrew from this week’s 3M Open
stories.summary: World number one golfer Scottie Scheffler says Jordan Spieth withdrew from the 3M Open to take a break after a grueling stretch of nine events in 11 weeks. Spieth is trying to get inside the top 50 of the FedEx Cup standings to secure a spot in next year's high-profile Signature Events. He currently stands at number 55 with two weeks remaining in the regular season. Spieth's decision to withdraw comes after a disappointing finish at last week's major, missing the cut at 10-over par.

stories.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
stories.summary: Tesla reported a 25 percent increase in vehicle deliveries to 480,126 in the second quarter of 2026, exceeding revenue expectations with $28.2 billion in revenue. The company's net income was $1.11 billion, a 5 percent increase from the same period last year. Despite a revenue boost, Tesla's profits remain relatively weak. The company's focus on delivering vehicles suggests its mission to become a leader in AI and robotics may be on hold for now.

stories.title: House Republicans Pass Stock Trading Bill Tied to SAVE Act and Voter ID Measure
stories.summary: The measure has drawn bipartisan backing amid a public outcry against the practice, but Democrats opposed it after Republicans added a voter ID requirement.

stories.title: Microsoft brings original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs
stories.summary: Microsoft has made four original Xbox titles playable on Windows PCs for the first time, starting with BLiNX: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fuzion Frenzy, available for $10 download or free with Xbox Game Pass subscription. The titles support enhanced graphics features, including Vsync and up to 4x resolution scaling. PC players can access these games starting today. This marks a significant expansion of Microsoft's backward compatibility effort beyond console ecosystems.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: Global oil prices surged to a 13-month high on Wednesday as US-Iran conflict escalated, with US forces continuing to attack Iran over its attempts to control shipping traffic in the Persian Gulf. The US has threatened to deepen the war effort, including targeting the Houthis. Oil prices have risen by 10% in the past week. The US has vowed to respond aggressively to any Iranian attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
stories.summary: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

stories.title: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
stories.summary: The Federal Communications Commission has eliminated a requirement for internet service providers to list all additional fees on broadband price labels, citing clutter and unnecessary detail. The change affects over 700,000 labels nationwide. The move follows complaints from ISPs about the burden of creating and updating labels to account for geographic variability. The labels will now only display the base monthly price.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted to $1,549.99 at Amazon and $1,599.99 at Best Buy, a $150 and $100 price drop respectively from its original $1,699 price. The device features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The deal is available now, with no end date specified.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to Nearly $120 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: Dunes Sagebrush Lizard Stripped of Endangered Status by Judge
stories.summary: A federal judge in Texas has stripped endangered species protections from the dunes sagebrush lizard, a small reptile found in southeastern New Mexico and West Texas. The lizard's habitat overlaps with the largest oil-producing region in the US. The decision affects approximately 1,500 square miles of land. The Biden administration's efforts to protect the lizard are now in question.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility is the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years. The event will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and media availability with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials. The wind tunnel is a major milestone in NASA's evolution, marking a significant step forward in the agency's research capabilities.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200%
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report highlights a flaw in decades of British food policy, specifically the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which unfairly targets fat. According to the evidence, sugar and refined carbohydrates are the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee is urging a new approach to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for reducing obesity.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
